nodes.label: Seven Prompt Safety Invariants
nodes.rationale: The audit verifies that translating processing prompts to English did not remove prompt-injection defenses, source validation, or secret-handling controls.

nodes.label: Lean Post-Work Turn
nodes.rationale: Keeping the coordinator's post-work turn compact reduces context-overflow risk after multi-agent fan-out.

nodes.label: Retrospective with Enforcement
nodes.rationale: Retrospective action items become GitHub Issues because production observations showed completion improving from 0% with Markdown checklists to 100% with issues.

nodes.label: SQL Tool Caveat
nodes.rationale: Cross-platform workflows must use filesystem state because the SQL tool is available only in CLI mode.

nodes.label: MCP Authentication Isolation
nodes.rationale: MCP servers often require service-specific credentials separate from existing CLI authentication, making authentication setup a documentation concern and a real integration blocker.

nodes.label: Cost First Unless Code
nodes.rationale: Cheap models handle non-code and mechanical work, while code and executable prompt design use standard-tier models where accuracy matters more.

nodes.label: Two-Layer State Model
nodes.rationale: Commit-scoped git notes keep agent context out of PR diffs, while the permanent state backend retains long-lived decisions and archives.

nodes.label: Per-Agent Note Namespaces
nodes.rationale: Separate namespaces prevent most concurrent note conflicts; shared research and review namespaces use append semantics.
